title: Stellaria tennesseensis
intro: Stellaria tennesseensis, also known as Tennessee starwort, is a perennial plant species in the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Stellaria tennesseensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stellaria tennesseensis is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Stellaria tennesseensis is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. This classification places it within the order Caryophyllales. The family Caryophyllaceae is a group of plants commonly known as the pink or carnation family. Stellaria tennesseensis is a perennial herb native to North America, and its classification reflects its relationship to other plants in the same family. The exact classification of Stellaria tennesseensis is still under review and may be subject to change.
